key_insight: Foundational MLIPs can substantially improve DFT formation energies toward experimental accuracy, and latent-feature delta-learning pushes errors below 50 meV/atom.
engineering_relevance: This provides a more accurate, open-source route to screen crystal stability and prioritize candidate materials without additional expensive DFT calculations.
method: Benchmarked MC3D formation energies against OQMD, Materials Project, and experimental enthalpies.
Used PET-OMATPES, a foundational MLIP trained at r2SCAN level, to correct GGA-based formation energies.
Trained classical delta-learning models on latent MLIP features to further refine predictions and preserve phase-stability trends.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports more than a 40% reduction in mean absolute error relative to GGA, with final errors below 50 meV/atom, comparable to experimental uncertainty.
watch_item: The correction quality may depend on the MLIP’s training domain and the regularization choice, so extrapolation to unfamiliar chemistries should be checked carefully.
paper_title: Correcting DFT formation energies towards experimental accuracy using foundational MLIPs and latent-feature delta-learning
